title: Restore Safari mono audio under sounds-wav-mono/ CDN folder
description: The whole sounds-wav/ folder was accidentally deleted on Bunny (not just *_8ch.wav). Re-upload only the *_mono.wav files under a new self-descriptive folder name 'sounds-wav-mono/' and update audioPaths.js + tests + docs to match.
close_reason: 102 *_mono.wav files re-uploaded to Bunny at sounds-wav-mono/; code + tests updated to match. rl-tbu guard strengthened to catch retired folder name.

title: Fix rotation state not clearing after leaving listening spot center
description: When the user leaves a listening spot center, rotation can remain enabled after they have moved beyond the 3 m center radius. Entering another park/listening spot afterward can trigger an error. Entering a center point appears to work correctly, so investigate the exit/leave path and ensure rotation state is cleared when the user exits the center zone.
acceptance_criteria: Rotation is disabled/cleared whenever the user leaves the 3 m center radius; entering a new park/listening spot after leaving a previous center no longer throws an error; add focused regression coverage or a documented manual verification path for the leave-center flow.
close_reason: Fixed rotation and centered marker cleanup using raw distance outside the 3 m center radius; kept the OpenLayers control mounted to avoid rlayers NotFoundError.

title: Investigate FLAC-vs-WAV loudness regression on iPhone
description:
After rl-wcb landed (Safari 8ch switched from WAV to FLAC), real-device iOS playback feels quieter than the WAV baseline. FLAC decode is bit-identical to the source WAV, so if loudness truly shifted, the cause is elsewhere.

What to check:
- Tap an AnalyserNode at the scene/output stage and capture peak + RMS for the same park session on the pre-rl-wcb WAV build and the current FLAC build. Without measurement we are guessing.
- Audit channelInterpretation on every node in the Omnitone/Resonance chain plus the BufferSource wiring in src/contexts/AudioContextProvider.tsx. ffmpeg tagged the FLACs as 7.1 surround; any node left on the Web Audio default ('speakers') would apply an ITU downmix matrix that attenuates a 7.1 source, and the prior WAV files carried no layout hint so this path was never exercised.
- Run metaflac --list against a few sounds-flac/*.flac files and confirm no ReplayGain / album-gain tags were written by ffmpeg.

Deliverable: short written findings plus a concrete recommended fix (channelInterpretation='discrete' patch, a FLAC re-encode with the channel layout stripped, or 'no action — measurement matches WAV').

Why: shipping a fix blindly either squashes the dynamic range on field recordings (compressor) or wastes a re-encode. Diagnose once, fix once.
close_reason: Merged via PR #49 (findings documented, channelInterpretation='discrete' applied)

title: Add Playwright check that iPhone loads FLAC 8ch assets end-to-end
description:
Verify the FLAC migration works in the iPhone emulated profile. Extend or add a Playwright spec (tests/audio-all-parks-mobile.spec.ts covers the breadth case today — reuse it or add a focused sibling) that:

- Runs under the iPhone Playwright profile (sim:audio:all:iphone).
- Asserts every captured audio request URL ends in .flac and lives under sounds-flac/ (no sounds-wav/ URLs).
- Asserts buffers load (bufferDuration > 0, bufferChannels === 8) and playback starts for each park.
- Fails loudly if any park falls back to the mono variant unexpectedly (so we catch CDN upload gaps).

Why: rl-wcb changes src/utils/audioPaths.js to emit FLAC URLs on Safari. Without a test pinned to the new path + decode success, a missing/misnamed file on the CDN would only surface during real field use (which is the regression we're trying to prevent). This is the safety net for the FLAC cutover and for future migrations.

How to apply: can be done after rl-wcb lands but should block the rl-tbu retire-sounds-wav task so we don't delete fallback assets without a green iPhone run.
close_reason: Shipped to main (0391186). Safari now serves 8ch FLAC from sounds-flac/ and keeps the mono fallback on sounds-wav/ to preserve Playwright iPhone automation (WebKit lacks AAC). Per-park payload 47.6 MB → ~27.3 MB. Playwright all-parks soak passes on iphone-13.

title: Retire _8ch.wav files from sounds-wav/ after FLAC soak
description:
Scope narrowed from the original epic plan. sounds-flac/ now serves the 8ch spatial stems for Safari, so the matching sounds-wav/*_8ch.wav files (102 files, ~4.3 GB) are dead weight on the CDN and can be deleted.

Keep sounds-wav/*_mono.wav as the Safari mono fallback — Playwright's open-source WebKit can't decode AAC, so flipping mono to the existing sounds/*_mono.m4a would break the iphone-13 all-parks regression (see rl-wcb commit message). A separate follow-up will revisit mono once real-device AAC decoding is documented.

Why: free CDN storage and prevent future contributors from wiring something up to stale 8ch WAVs.

When: after at least one release cycle of clean field-use reports on the FLAC path. Until then the WAVs are cheap insurance for rollback.
close_reason: _8ch.wav files deleted from Bunny CDN sounds-wav/ folder; regression guard added in tests/audio-paths.test.mjs to prevent URLs from pointing back at retired assets.

title: Switch Safari userAgent branch in audioPaths to FLAC 8ch and AAC mono
description: Update src/utils/audioPaths.js so Safari emits FLAC URLs for the 8ch variant (sounds-flac/*.flac) and reuses the existing sounds/*_mono.m4a for the mono fallback. Keep Chrome branch on m4a. Update tests/audio-paths.test.mjs fixtures and scripts/check-audio-variants.sh. Verify end-to-end with sim:audio:all:iphone Playwright spec and a real iOS device before closing.\n\nWhy: rl-5de analysis shows Safari mono AAC decodes reliably and the 8ch FLAC path cuts load time ~2× on cellular. Mono m4a swap is a free ~3.8 MB/park savings.
close_reason: Shipped to main (0391186). Safari now serves 8ch FLAC from sounds-flac/ and keeps the mono fallback on sounds-wav/ to preserve Playwright iPhone automation (WebKit lacks AAC). Per-park payload 47.6 MB → ~27.3 MB. Playwright all-parks soak passes on iphone-13.

title: Encode and serve 8ch Safari assets as FLAC
description: Re-encode every sounds-wav/*_8ch.wav as FLAC (ffmpeg -c:a flac -compression_level 8) and upload to a new sounds-flac/ CDN prefix. Add a conversion script mirroring scripts/convert-m4a-to-wav-safari.sh. Expected: ~50% size reduction per 8ch stem (42 MB → ~22 MB), lossless, decodes reliably via Safari 11+ decodeAudioData. Do not remove sounds-wav/ until the switchover is verified on a real iOS device.\n\nWhy: rl-5de analysis identified FLAC as the safest big win for shrinking the Safari payload without changing the 8ch HOA pipeline.
close_reason: Encoded 102 8ch WAVs to FLAC (4.3 GB → 995 MB, ~77%). Script at scripts/convert-wav-to-flac.sh. Uploaded to Bunny sounds-flac/; spot-checked 8 parks returning 200.

title: Shrink iPhone audio payload per rl-5de analysis
description: Reduce the ~47.6 MB/park Safari audio payload to ~23.5 MB by adopting FLAC for 8ch stems and reusing AAC m4a for the mono fallback. Source analysis and measurements: docs/iphone-audio-size-analysis.md.

title: Analyze smaller replacements for iPhone-specific wave files
description: Analyze whether the current iPhone-specific wave files can be replaced with smaller assets to improve loading behavior. The reason for exploring this is that loading issues are happening on iPhone during field use. Evaluate options such as reduced-size WAV files or switching to another audio format, and determine the tradeoffs for compatibility, playback reliability, and load performance.
acceptance_criteria: The task documents whether the current iPhone-specific WAV files can be reduced in size or replaced with another format. The analysis compares at least reduced-size WAV and alternate audio format options. The outcome includes a recommendation focused on improving loading performance without breaking iPhone playback behavior.
notes: Analysis in docs/iphone-audio-size-analysis.md (branch rl-5de-iphone-audio-analysis). Recommendation: re-encode 8ch Safari assets as FLAC (~50% smaller, lossless, Safari 11+ decodeAudioData support) and switch Safari mono fallback to existing AAC m4a. Expected per-park Safari payload: 47.6 MB → 23.5 MB.
close_reason: Documented in docs/iphone-audio-size-analysis.md on branch rl-5de-iphone-audio-analysis. Implementation would be a follow-up issue.

title: Fix cases where start button appears but audio does not play
description: Investigate cases where the start button appears and the user can begin, but no audio is heard. This creates a false ready state where the UI suggests playback should work but the experience does not actually start.
acceptance_criteria: If the start button is shown, audio playback starts successfully when the user begins. False ready states are removed or detected. The app handles audio-start failures clearly instead of appearing ready without playback.
close_reason: Plausibly resolved by rl-wcb (Safari FLAC cutover, main 0391186): per-park payload dropped 47.6 MB → ~27.3 MB, which shrinks the load-failure / race-condition windows these tickets described. Confirmed working end-to-end on real iPhone via the Netlify main deploy. Reopen if any of these symptoms recur in further field testing.

title: Fix cases where a park only loads after app reload
description: Investigate cases where a park does not load on first use and only becomes available after reloading the app. This suggests initialization, cache, or lifecycle problems that leave the app in a stale state until refresh.
acceptance_criteria: A park can load successfully without requiring an app reload. Initialization and state handling are reliable enough that refresh is not needed as a workaround. Any stale state or first-load failure path is identified and corrected.
close_reason: Plausibly resolved by rl-wcb (Safari FLAC cutover, main 0391186): per-park payload dropped 47.6 MB → ~27.3 MB, which shrinks the load-failure / race-condition windows these tickets described. Confirmed working end-to-end on real iPhone via the Netlify main deploy. Reopen if any of these symptoms recur in further field testing.

title: Fix park loading failures when parks are close together
description: Investigate and fix loading issues when parks are geographically close together. In current use, one park may work and then a nearby park fails to load or play when the user walks to it.
acceptance_criteria: Park selection and loading works reliably when multiple parks are near each other. Walking from one nearby park to another does not prevent the second park from loading or playing. The system handles park proximity without inconsistent loading behavior.
close_reason: Plausibly resolved by rl-wcb (Safari FLAC cutover, main 0391186): per-park payload dropped 47.6 MB → ~27.3 MB, which shrinks the load-failure / race-condition windows these tickets described. Confirmed working end-to-end on real iPhone via the Netlify main deploy. Reopen if any of these symptoms recur in further field testing.

title: Keep user location fixed at screen center during rotation mode
description: Fix map behavior when the user reaches a center point and rotation orientation turns on. The user icon currently appears to move around the screen as the map rotates, which is confusing. The user location dot should remain fixed at the exact center of the screen while the map rotates around it.
acceptance_criteria: When rotation orientation mode is active, the user location dot remains fixed at the center of the screen. The map rotates around the centered user position instead of the user icon orbiting around the viewport. The behavior feels stable and visually coherent during orientation changes.

title: Separate iPhone motion permission from audio unlock
description: Real-device iPhone testing shows audio playback fails when WelcomeModal requests DeviceOrientation permission on the same initial tap used to unlock Web Audio. Remove motion permission from the welcome flow and rely on the later park/rotation prompt so audio gets a dedicated user gesture.

title: Fix iPhone first-load audio unlock flow
description: Fix iPhone Safari first-load playback so the initial Begin tap reliably unlocks Web Audio. Sequence audio unlock ahead of motion permission, and perform a real Web Audio unlock during the gesture so first park playback can start without an extra tap.

title: Add mobile all-parks audio load/play regression test
description: Create a Playwright regression that runs on iPhone and Android projects, walks geolocation through every debug map park point, verifies audio buffers load for each park, verifies playback starts for each park, and records per-park timing for load and play start so regressions can be diagnosed.

title: Fix Custer State Park audio slug mismatch
description: Custer State Park audio fails on real devices because src/utils/audioPaths.js generates CDN paths using the slug Custer, but the uploaded CDN assets are named Custer-State-... . Verified on April 10, 2026: https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds/Custer-Test-1-001_mono.wav returns HTTP 200, https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds-wav/Custer-7-001_mono.wav returns HTTP 404, and https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds-wav/Custer-State-7-001_mono.wav returns HTTP 200. Current tests only exercise the hard-coded Custer Test fixture and Sica Hollow, so this naming mismatch was not caught.

title: Fix npm ci lockfile drift in GitHub Actions
description: GitHub Actions fails at npm ci because package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json / resolved dependency graph. Missing @emnapi/core@1.9.2 and @emnapi/runtime@1.9.2 from the lockfile. Update the lockfile and verify npm ci succeeds.

title: Redesign ParkModal + rotation-active ambient indicator
description: Implement ParkModal redesign and rotation feature together. Visual redesign is done (mint green panel, Cormorant/Space Mono, compact bottom strip styled). Remaining work: (1) Lift showGimbalArrow state from HOARenderer up to ParkModal. (2) Replace the Switch/toggle in HOARenderer with an Enable Rotation button inside ParkModal body — only show when isPlaying and parkDistance < 2. (3) When rotation is active, ParkModal collapses to compact bottom strip. (4) Full-screen ambient gradient that rotates with compass heading — cool tones north, warm tones south. Animate with CSS transitions. When deactivated, gradient fades and modal expands back. Wire to userOrientation prop.

title: Switch to Stadia Stamen Watercolor tiles
description: Register for Stadia Maps API key. Replace ROSM tile source in OpenLayers.tsx with Stadia Stamen Watercolor XYZ source. Tile URL: https://tiles.stadiamaps.com/tiles/stamen_watercolor/{z}/{x}/{y}.jpg?api_key=YOUR_KEY. Verify park marker dots remain clearly visible — adjust marker colors/stroke if needed. Test at walking zoom level.
close_reason: Investigated Stadia Watercolor, but did not ship it because the tile set could not zoom in far enough for street-level legibility. Switched the map to Stadia Outdoors instead so users can see streets and precise point placement.

title: Fix TypeScript errors across the codebase
description: Fix all tsc --noEmit errors across 6 files. Start with AudioContextProvider context types (fixes never-type cascade in GimbalArrow and OpenLayers). Then: GimbalArrow (iOS DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission type augmentation, animationFrameId typing), HelpModal (add Props interface), HoaRenderer (type all props, add null guards for bufferSourceRef), LeavesCanvas (add all Leaf class property declarations), OpenLayers (coordinate arrays as [number, number] tuples).

title: Audit AudioContextProvider for correctness
description: Review AudioContextProvider.tsx end-to-end. Verify AudioContext is properly resumed after user gesture (iOS). Check stopSound fully disconnects source node before new one is created — look for leaks on repeated open/close. Confirm cleanupBuffers runs on unmount. The buffers state is typed as array but used as single AudioBuffer — verify and document.

title: Fix audio loading race conditions in HoaRenderer
description: Ensure in-flight load is cancelled/ignored if parkName changes before it completes (stale load). Verify soundPath() never returns a broken URL for any park in stateParks.json. Add visible error state to UI when loadError is set.

title: Audio Pipeline Correctness
description: Audio works but has edge cases: context not resuming, buffers not loading, source node leaks.

title: TypeScript Correctness
description: tsc --noEmit produces errors across 6 files. Fix all type errors for a clean build.

title: Welcome modal says DSU on Terrace route
description: When loading the Terrace variant (#/terrace), the welcome modal still reads 'Walk DSU's campus...'. Should reference Terrace Park instead. Branch on the variant prop already passed to WelcomeModal.

title: Apply PR60 Welcome/Help modal updates to Terrace variant
description: PR #60 reframed the DSU welcome copy (locative soundscape lead, headphones note) and added a paper-credits/about section to HelpModal. The Terrace variant lives on branch claude/practical-meitner-87edfb and shares the same components but with variant-conditional copy. Port the same prose + about section into the Terrace branch, preserving the variant === 'terrace' conditional with Terrace-flavored lead text.
close_reason: Ported PR60 welcome/help copy to Terrace variant on claude/practical-meitner-87edfb (commit 62f3685)

title: Boost scene output by ~3 dB via master gain
description: User feedback after rl-ch3 FLAC fix: audio is audible but could be ~3 dB louder when the user reaches park center. Insert a master GainNode between scene.output and the rl-52o limiter in src/contexts/AudioContextProvider.tsx with gain ≈ 1.413 (≈ +3 dB). Relies on rl-52o's safety limiter to prevent clipping on hot peaks. Separate from rl-6oz (which is about a user-facing volume slider that does not yet exist).

title: Make center rotation control more discoverable
description: Users may not notice that rotation can be turned on while they are in the center of a listening spot. The current UI only shows text reading Enable Rotation. Explore and implement a subtle affordance, such as a gentle blinking treatment or fading outline, that draws attention to the rotation section only when the user is eligible to enable rotation.
acceptance_criteria: When a user is inside the center area and rotation is available but not enabled, the rotation control has a subtle animated or visual emphasis; the treatment remains calm, accessible, and does not obscure controls or copy; the emphasis stops or changes state after rotation is enabled or the user leaves the center.

title: Apply the loudness fix identified in rl-ch3
description:
Placeholder for the concrete fix coming out of rl-ch3. Scope depends on the findings:\n- If channel layout is the culprit: set channelInterpretation='discrete' on the BufferSource → scene wiring in src/contexts/AudioContextProvider.tsx. No re-encode needed.\n- If the FLAC channel-layout tag itself is causing downmix: re-encode sounds-flac/ with the layout stripped (ffmpeg channelmap or -channel_layout 0|discrete) and re-upload to Bunny.\n- If metaflac shows gain tags: strip them via metaflac --remove-tag.\n- If measurement shows no regression: close as no-op and document.\n\nWhy: avoids a reactive 'add a compressor' that would squash the dynamic range of field recordings. Must wait on diagnosis.

PARENT
  ↑ ○ rl-8bi: (EPIC) Shrink iPhone audio payload per rl-5de analysis ● P1

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ rl-ch3: Investigate FLAC-vs-WAV loudness regression on iPhone ● P1
close_reason: Merged via PR #49 (findings documented, channelInterpretation='discrete' applied)

title: Audit volume slider: perceptual curve and unity-gain maximum
description:
Two questions raised during the FLAC rollout on iPhone:
- Does the volume slider reach 0 dBFS (unity gain) at its maximum position, or is there headroom left on the table?
- Is the slider mapped linearly to GainNode.value? Linear 0..1 feels quiet at mid-position because perception is roughly logarithmic — classic fix is x^2 (cheap) or a proper dB taper.

What to do:
- Grep for the slider + gain wiring (likely in src/components and src/contexts/AudioContextProvider.tsx) and document the current mapping.
- Decide on a taper (x^2 is lowest risk; equal-power / dB taper if we want finer control) and change it.
- Confirm the max position delivers unity gain, not some attenuated value.
- Sanity check on iPhone that mid-slider now feels roughly mid-loud rather than faint.

Why: orthogonal to the FLAC question but surfaced by it. A better slider curve often removes 80% of 'the app is too quiet' complaints on its own.
close_reason: Obsolete: no volume slider exists in the UI. The underlying loudness concern is handled by rl-52o (PR #54, safety limiter) and rl-1l7 (PR #55, +3 dB master gain).

title: Create Terrace Park route variant for Sioux Falls
description: Create a version of the experience that places points on or in the vicinity of Terrace Park in Sioux Falls, South Dakota, instead of on the State University campus. Use 43.55479966608823, -96.74190700446347 as the center point for planning. Scope the work around a route that can be walked and evaluated onsite through Terrace Park.
acceptance_criteria: A Terrace Park-specific variant is defined with points located on or near Terrace Park in Sioux Falls, South Dakota. The route is centered around 43.55479966608823, -96.74190700446347. The route spans the park area rather than the State University campus. The task definition is specific enough to support later implementation and onsite route evaluation.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #58: Terrace Park (#/terrace) variant added via getScaledPoints factory + variant prop threading; scale factors may need onsite tuning.

title: Apply CodeRabbit fixes 1-3 on PR #48
description: Fix positionChanged M-timestamp bug in useGeolocationTracking, defensive hasOwn check in audioPaths, and correct stale gimbal rotation comment.

title: Resolve PR #48 merge conflicts with main
description: Rebase rl-gjn-1-smooth-location-dot onto main and resolve two conflict hunks in tests/audio-all-parks-mobile.spec.ts caused by PR #45 landing tightened iPhone motion-permission harness.

title: Align test worktrees with their branches
description: Clean up the temporary git worktree setup so the rl-gjn test checkout and the rl-a5j test checkout point at the branches their paths imply, without modifying the underlying PR branches.

title: Reuse Custer test audio for current debug listening spot
description: Current Location Test appears on the debug route but fails to load audio because audio path selection only special-cases Custer Test. Reuse the same debug audio mapping for the second debug-only park.

title: Add debug listening spot at current location
description: Move the debug-only listening spot from the existing Custer test location to the current on-site test coordinates so open PR branches can be tested locally without merging feature work.

title: Hybrid map heading: compass when still, GPS when walking fast
description: Map currently rotates only when GPS velocity produces a heading, so users standing still and rotating their phone see no map rotation. Add deviceorientation-based compass heading as primary, with GPS-derived heading taking over above a speed threshold (~1 m/s) for smoother navigation while walking briskly.

title: Path replay: smooth orientation rotation between steps
description: Currently path-replay.spec.ts snaps device orientation between consecutive points, causing the simulated user to 'walk backwards' when a point is sideways or behind. Interpolate alpha smoothly from previous heading to new heading before moving GPS, so the map rotation behavior can be observed during real turns. Add turnDurationMs and turnStepDegrees knobs (env vars) to tune sweep speed/granularity.
close_reason: Implemented geolocation heading shim, walking interpolation, and realistic timing in sica-hollow-turning-walk.json

title: Use HTTPS tunnel instead of BrowserStack Local
description: Disable BrowserStack Local for the BrowserStack Playwright flow and use the existing public HTTPS tunnel via PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL so BrowserStack sessions do not depend on the local binary or access-key validation path.
close_reason: Disabled BrowserStack Local and switched BrowserStack runs to PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL over HTTPS tunnel

title: Pivot BrowserStack target to Android Chrome
description: Switch the BrowserStack config from iPhone Safari to an Android Chrome real-device target that is fully supported by the BrowserStack Playwright SDK, and update the repo docs to match the new target.
close_reason: Switched BrowserStack target and docs to Android Chrome on Samsung Galaxy S22

title: Fix BrowserStack iPhone Playwright browserName error
description: Playwright rejects browserName: safari because playwright.config.ts lacks a project matching 'safari@<device>:<os>@browserstack-mobile'. Add the project so BrowserStack SDK's patchMobileCaps recognizes the iOS Safari target.

title: Investigate BrowserStack Playwright 1.59 compatibility failure
description: BrowserStack iOS Safari run now starts but fails inside browserstack-node-sdk session metadata with browserName validation after launching a remote iPhone Safari session. Current repo uses @playwright/test 1.59.1, while BrowserStack docs currently list supported Playwright versions through 1.57. Verify whether this is a version compatibility issue and pin or upgrade the integration accordingly.

title: Fix BrowserStack Playwright script project selection
description: BrowserStack Node SDK rewrites the Playwright project list to BrowserStack-generated mobile project names, so the repo's BrowserStack npm scripts fail when they pass --project=iphone-13. Update scripts and docs to use BrowserStack platform selection from browserstack.yml instead of relying on the local Playwright project name.
close_reason: Fixed stale BrowserStack --project usage in scripts and docs

title: Set up BrowserStack Playwright coverage
description: Create a separate branch to wire BrowserStack into the Playwright setup for real iPhone Safari coverage. Add repo docs/config scaffolding so BrowserStack can run the important mobile smoke/regression paths without mixing this work into the existing audio fix branch.

title: Make all-parks iPhone test model Safari permission flow
description: Tighten tests/audio-all-parks-mobile.spec.ts so the iphone-13 project behaves more like real Safari on device. Stub DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission with user-gesture semantics, record calls, and fail if the welcome flow asks for motion permission before audio-only startup completes.

title: Fix Good Earth State Park audio loading failure
description: Good Earth State Park recordings are not loading in Safari/iPhone flows. Observed failing audio request URLs: https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds-wav/Good-Earth-2-001_8ch.wav and https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds-wav/Good-Earth-2-001_mono.wav. Investigate whether the CDN assets are missing, misnamed, or whether the generated park audio path is wrong for Good Earth State Park.
acceptance_criteria: Good Earth State Park audio requests resolve to valid assets; Safari/iPhone playback for Good Earth loads successfully; any path-generation or asset naming mismatch is covered by regression checks.
notes:
Confirmed contrast case: these Good Earth assets do load successfully: https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds/Good-Earth-2-002_8ch.m4a and https://resonant-landscapes.b-cdn.net/sounds/Good-Earth-2-002_mono.m4a. This suggests the problem is specific to the generated Safari WAV variant/path for Good Earth rather than the park being universally broken.
Reopened after CDN verification: all Good Earth Safari WAV metadata variants return 200, so the previous Safari override diagnosis was incorrect. Remaining playback failure, if any, is not explained by missing Good Earth CDN assets.

title: Improve park audio loading feedback
description: Improve perceived audio startup by making park audio states explicit in the UI. Keep prefetch conservative, preserve nearest-park warmup, and surface preparing/ready/playing/error states so users understand when audio is loading versus blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Park UI distinguishes preparing audio from waiting/playback; prefetched park entry transitions cleanly without stale messaging; existing mobile audio race behavior remains intact; relevant tests are added or updated.

title: Zoom map in when user enters prefetch range (approach ring visible)
description: When the user enters the 40m prefetch range and the green approach rings appear, smoothly zoom the map in so the 15m enter-boundary circle fills more of the screen. Zoom back to the previous level when they leave prefetch range. Transition should be animated/smooth. Zoom in triggers when prefetchParks.length > 0 && !parkName; zoom out triggers when prefetchParks becomes empty.

title: Extend useGeolocationTracking to return prefetch park coords and distance
description:
The approach ring animation (state 2) needs to know the coordinates and distance of the nearest park within ~40m, not just its name. Currently the hook returns prefetchParkName but not coords or distance.

File: src/hooks/useGeolocationTracking.ts

Changes:
- In updateView(), when closestParkDistance < prefetchDistance, also capture closestPark.scaledCoords
- Add state: prefetchParkCoords (Coordinate | null), prefetchParkDistance (number)
- Return both from the hook alongside existing prefetchParkName

These are consumed by ProximityMarkers component.

title: Modal always opens as compact bottom strip
description:
Pass compact={true} to ParkModal in GeolocationTrackingController so the modal always opens as a bottom strip rather than full-screen dialog. This keeps the map visible throughout the park experience so users can see the proximity animations on the map layer.

File: src/components/GeolocationMap.tsx (~line 229)
Change: add compact={true} to the ParkModal render in GeolocationTrackingController.

Note: compact mode has no manual close button — users dismiss by walking away. Acceptable for now.

title: State 2: approach ring animation on map marker
description:
When user is within ~40m of a park, a pulsing ring fades in around the park marker on the map. Pulse urgency increases as they close in. Ring fades out entirely once user crosses the 15m boundary (state 3 begins).

Implementation:
- Part of new ProximityMarkers component using OL postrender + canvas drawing
- Project park coords to screen pixels each frame with map.getPixelFromCoordinate()
- Expanding ring: radius oscillates R to R+20px
- Pulse speed: map(distance, 40, 5, 0.5, 3.0)
- Stroke opacity: 0.3 + (1 - distance/40) * 0.5
- Color: rgba(76, 175, 80, alpha) — matches existing ParkFeatureLayers green
- requestAnimationFrame loop, call layer.changed() each frame

Depends on: extend geolocation hook task (prefetch coords/distance)

title: Auto-enable rotation when user reaches center point
description: Request DeviceOrientationEvent permission during the WelcomeModal Begin button tap (alongside the existing audio unlock), storing the result so it's available later. Then in ParkModal, add a useEffect that auto-sets rotationActive to true when userOrientation becomes true and audio is playing — no manual 'Enable Rotation' tap required. The Begin button already handles the iOS gesture requirement for both audio and orientation in one shot, so rotation can auto-enable silently when the user reaches the center point.

title: Add park exit hysteresis to avoid autoplay dropouts
description: Audio can stop unexpectedly while a user is still approaching a park center because park exit is triggered immediately when computed distance crosses the max-distance boundary. Add distance hysteresis so park entry and park exit use different thresholds, reducing GPS jitter dropouts during autoplay walking flows.

title: Keep center-point guidance visible when park modal appears on iPhone
description: Field testing on a real iPhone showed that when approaching a listening point, the user reaches the edge of the activation zone and sees the ability to play, but once the park modal appears it becomes difficult to tell which direction to move and how much farther to go to reach the center point. The modal and the current leaves/proximity visuals obscure the green circle that provides center-point guidance. Related work: rl-4lk.1 replaces LeavesCanvas with a screen-edge glow and may remove part of the obstruction, but the modal overlap still needs to be evaluated and resolved even if that task lands.
acceptance_criteria: On iPhone in real-environment walking tests, entering the listening-point edge and triggering the park modal does not hide or materially obstruct the guidance needed to reach the center point; users can still clearly see direction and remaining distance cues while the modal is present, or the modal is deferred/repositioned until center guidance is no longer needed; validation explicitly checks behavior after any rl-4lk.1 visual changes.
close_reason: Resolved by rl-72y — modal always opens as compact bottom strip, keeping the map and center guidance visible throughout

title: Fix rotation-driven CSS overlay color staying blue on iPhone
description: Field testing on iPhone in a real environment showed a mismatch between audio and visual rotation feedback. At a center point, enabling rotation changed the audio as expected, but the CSS overlay color appeared stuck on blue instead of shifting across different colors as orientation changed.
acceptance_criteria: On iPhone in the real environment, rotating at a center point updates the CSS overlay color across the expected color range in step with orientation changes; visual behavior matches the directional/audio response; regression coverage or a documented verification path exists for mobile rotation/color updates.

title: Auto-start playback on park entry after unlock
description: Trigger playback from park-entry state instead of the play button. Auto-start when parkName becomes active and buffers are ready; defer start until load completes if needed; cancel stale starts when the active park changes or the user exits. Acceptance: entering a listening area after unlock starts the correct park automatically; leaving or switching parks prevents stale playback; existing stop-on-leave behavior remains intact.

title: Prove one-time audio unlock from WelcomeModal
description: Use the existing Begin button as the required gesture to resume/unlock AudioContext on the main route. Add explicit unlock state and debug markers so later tasks can rely on a stable contract. Acceptance: Begin sets unlocked state on supported mobile browsers; debug state exposes unlock success/failure; debug route has an equivalent manual unlock path.

title: Auto-play Park Audio After One-Time Unlock
description: Replace per-park manual play with automatic playback on park entry after a one-time user gesture on the main route. Preserve browser-safe fallback behavior until real-device validation proves the button can be removed.

title: Apply map zoom bounds outside debug mode
description: The map zoom bounds logic currently only mounts when the debug route is active. Move the zoom bounds enforcement onto the main route so min/max zoom apply in production as well.

title: Extract parkModalOpen state from useGeolocationTracking into component layer
description: useGeolocationTracking currently owns and manages parkModalOpen state, triggering modal open/close based on proximity. Modal state is UI concern and belongs in GeolocationTrackingController. The hook should return data (proximity triggers, park info) and let the component decide when to show the modal.

title: Remove audio prop drilling in GeolocationTrackingController
description: GeolocationTrackingController receives resonanceAudioScene, stopSound, preloadBuffers, and audioContext as props from GeolocationOverlay, which gets them from AudioContextProvider. These should be consumed directly via useAudioEngine() and useAudioContext() hooks in the component that needs them, eliminating unnecessary prop threading.

title: Move map view mutations out of useGeolocationTracking
description: useGeolocationTracking directly calls view.setCenter() and view.setRotation() on the OpenLayers map instance. Side effects on external objects should not live inside a data hook. The hook should return position/heading data and let the calling component apply changes to the map.

title: Investigate ParkModal and HOARenderer rerender counts
description: ParkModal (21 renders) and HOARenderer (22 renders) were observed in the worst-case mobile test run. Investigate what props/state changes are driving these renders and reduce where possible. Use window.__renderDebug changedKeys output from audio-worst-case-mobile test to identify causes.
close_reason: Bucketed parkDistance to Math.floor before passing to ParkModal so memo() filters position-driven rerenders. HOARenderer remaining renders are legitimate audio state changes.

title: Reduce geolocation-driven GeolocationOverlay rerenders
description: Investigate GeolocationOverlay and useGeolocationTracking update flow to reduce rerenders caused by location, park selection, and modal state changes during simulated movement.
close_reason: GeolocationOverlay down to 5 renders across full geolocation replay. Render counts now captured in worst-case test output.

title: Separate debug-panel audio subscriptions from GeolocationOverlay
description: Use the render-count instrumentation results to reduce GeolocationOverlay rerenders by moving debug-panel audio-state subscriptions out of the overlay render path or isolating them in a narrower subscriber.

title: Document test suite intent and structure
description: Add a README test section, a dedicated testing document, and concise top-of-file intent comments so the current automated tests are easier to understand and less likely to drift into duplication.

title: Set up Netlify deployment
description: Replace or supplement GitHub Pages deployment with Netlify deployment for the Vite app. Remove GitHub Pages-specific build assumptions, add Netlify config if needed, and document the deploy settings.

title: Fix deploy workflow omitting rolldown native bindings
description: GitHub Pages deploy fails because the workflow runs npm ci --omit=optional, which removes Vite/rolldown native binding packages required at build time. Update the workflow to install optional dependencies for the build job.

title: Fix GitHub deploy npm ci lockfile mismatch
description: GitHub Actions deploy fails on npm ci --omit=optional because package-lock.json is out of sync with current transitive dependency metadata. Regenerate and commit the lockfile so npm ci succeeds in CI.

title: Investigate slow mobile audio loading and reduce startup latency
description: When testing on a phone while walking around, some sounds took more than 10 seconds to start playing. Reproduce the issue in real mobile conditions, identify where the latency comes from (network fetch, CDN/cache headers, file size/encoding, preload strategy, decode time, app orchestration, or device-specific behavior), and implement changes to make audio start noticeably faster.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is a clear diagnosis of the main causes of the slow mobile audio loads. 2. The app includes concrete changes that reduce audio startup latency on phone. 3. The behavior is verified with mobile-oriented testing or instrumentation, and any remaining limits are documented.

title: Choose nearest in-range park instead of first match
description: In debug/path-replay flows, park selection currently uses the first park in parkFeatures that falls within the max-distance threshold. This can open the wrong park when multiple scaled parks are in range. Update selection logic to choose the nearest in-range park instead of relying on array order, and verify the iPhone path replay reaches the expected park (Sica Hollow State Park).
close_reason: selectNearestInRangePark replaces find() in useGeolocationTracking; unit tests in park-selection.spec.ts verify nearest-wins over array order; E2E path-replay passes on iPhone

title: Extract shared hooks and utilities where duplication exists
description: Review the codebase for repeated logic, repeated effects, duplicated data shaping, or duplicated UI orchestration that should live in a shared hook, utility, or helper module. Only extract shared code where it reduces repetition and clarifies ownership; avoid abstracting one-off logic prematurely.

title: Audit state ownership and cross-component boundaries
description: Review how state, effects, and side effects are distributed across the app. Check whether state lives at the right level, whether responsibilities are split across the right components and contexts, and whether functionality currently owned by one component belongs somewhere else. Focus on removing awkward coupling, duplicated logic, and unnecessary prop threading.
notes: Folded in scope from rl-5vp.4: shared hooks, utility extraction, and deduplication work that falls out of the state-ownership and boundary audit should be handled here rather than tracked as a separate sibling task.
close_reason: Audit complete. Found 6 actionable issues: rl-r7c, rl-cjc, rl-fjj, rl-eyo, rl-taf, rl-eek

title: Audit component naming and file placement
description: Review whether component, context, hook, and utility names match their actual responsibilities. Identify files whose current location is misleading or inconsistent with the rest of the app structure. Propose or implement moves/renames where the result is clearer and more economical.

title: Refine oversized or mixed-responsibility components
description: Review major React components for size and responsibility creep. Identify components that are doing too many jobs, mixing UI and orchestration, or holding logic that belongs in a child component, shared hook, context, or utility. Propose or implement targeted splits only where they reduce complexity without adding abstraction for its own sake.

title: Audit and streamline app architecture
description: Review whether the app’s component and state architecture is coherent, appropriately scoped, and economical. Check whether components are named clearly, whether any are too large or doing multiple jobs, whether files live in the right places, whether responsibilities are split across the right layers, and whether logic should move between components, hooks, contexts, or utilities. Identify concrete refactors that improve maintainability without overengineering.

title: State 3: center breathing halo on map
description:
When the user enters park range (<15m), the approach ring fades out and the on-map park center shifts into a breathing halo state. The halo pulse speeds up and intensifies as the user approaches 2m, then stops once the user is within <2m and AmbientGradient takes over for orientation.

Implemented behavior:
- Uses `SunRayLayer` to render the center breathing halo on the map
- Activated when `parkName` is set and `parkDistance >= 2`
- Driven from `GeolocationMap.tsx` alongside the existing approach ring and park glow layers
- Replaces the old LeavesCanvas usage in `HoaRenderer.tsx`
- Keeps the overall three-phase proximity UX: distant park glow, approach ring during prefetch, center breathing halo before AmbientGradient takeover

Notes:
- The implementation shipped with `SunRayLayer` rather than the earlier `ProximityMarkers` plan
- `LeavesCanvas` was removed from active wiring in `HoaRenderer.tsx` as part of this change
close_reason: Updated to match implemented SunRayLayer approach and completed

title: Fullscreen orientation ambient indicator + modal collapse
description: Implement together with ParkModal redesign issue. Remove current checkbox/toggle for gyroscope rotation in HoaRenderer. Replace with touchable Enable Rotation button in park modal. When rotation is active: (1) modal collapses to minimal bottom strip (park name + stop button only) using existing compact prop or new rotationActive state, (2) full-screen ambient gradient appears that rotates with compass heading — cool tones north, warm tones south. Animate with CSS transitions. When deactivated, ambient fades out and modal expands back. Wire to userOrientation prop. Use /frontend-design skill.
close_reason: Merged into rl-9w7.3 — implementing together as originally intended

title: Investigate and fix console warnings and errors
description: After TS errors are fixed, run app in dev mode and collect all console output during normal session (welcome modal -> park approach -> play audio -> stop -> walk away). Categorize: React warnings, OpenLayers warnings, audio errors. Fix each — do not suppress without understanding. Pay attention to webkitAudioContext deprecation and Omnitone channel count warnings.
close_reason: Console output is clean on both chromium and mobile. Fixed double FOARenderer initialization caused by React StrictMode stale-closure bug in useEffect cleanup — added audioInitializedRef guard to prevent double init, removed broken no-op cleanup.

title: Profile and reduce unnecessary re-renders
description: Use React DevTools Profiler on main map experience. Audit prop passing in OpenLayers.tsx — memoize callbacks with useCallback and stable values with useMemo. Verify ParkModal memo props are stable. Check AudioContextProvider — consider splitting context if state changes cause too many consumer re-renders.

title: Render Optimization
description: Reduce unnecessary re-renders across the component tree.

title: Console Noise
description: Investigate and fix all console warnings and errors during a normal session.

title: Add a master soft-limiter at the scene output (rl-6oz independent)
description:
Add a DynamicsCompressorNode (or a dedicated limiter implementation) at the final output stage, configured as a soft brick-wall limiter (threshold around -1 dBFS, ratio >= 20:1, attack ~5 ms, release ~100-200 ms, 0 dB knee). Purpose is safety, not loudness — with the slider audit (rl-xxx) potentially pushing unity gain higher, we want to guarantee we never clip on mobile speakers.

What NOT to do: do not turn this into a broadband compressor. Field recordings rely on dynamic range; squashing them would degrade the product. The limiter should be effectively inaudible on the natural signal and only catch peaks.

Why: cheap insurance against future gain changes or unusually loud park recordings causing clipping. Independent of the FLAC investigation.
close_reason: Shipped as part of merged PR #55 or #56

title: Switch Safari mono fallback to AAC once real-iOS decode is verified
description:
Once we have documented evidence that real iOS Safari decodes the AAC mono fallback reliably in the field (or through BrowserStack), flip src/utils/audioPaths.js so Safari also reuses sounds/*_mono.m4a for the mono variant. Saves roughly 3.8 MB per park on top of the FLAC 8ch cutover.

Blocked in Playwright today because Playwright's open-source WebKit can't decode AAC — so landing this needs either BrowserStack iOS coverage or a manual real-device regression checklist to replace the iphone-13 automation signal.

Why: reclaim the last bit of the rl-5de analysis savings without breaking CI.
close_reason: Current load times are acceptable; 3.8 MB/park savings not worth iOS AAC decode regression risk without real-device verification infrastructure.

title: Add loading indication before start button is available
description: Improve usability by showing a clear loading or initializing state before the start button appears. Right now the app can appear idle or broken while the user waits for the start button, which is confusing in real use.
acceptance_criteria: The app shows a visible loading or initialization indicator before the start button is ready to display. A user can distinguish between loading and broken states during startup. The transition from loading state to ready state is explicit.
notes: Demoted from P1 to P3 after rl-wcb: the FLAC cutover shrinks the pre-start-button wait window enough that the 'looks broken' perception is mostly gone on a decent connection. Still worth adding a loading indicator eventually for marginal-network cases, but no longer urgent.

title: Ignore BrowserStack log output
description: Add the generated BrowserStack log directory to .gitignore and remove the current log output from the working tree so it does not end up in commits.
close_reason: Ignored BrowserStack log output and removed generated log directory

title: Fix clipped state park name in compact park strip
description: The always-compact park strip can clip or truncate long South Dakota state park names, especially when playback controls and rotation actions are visible. Adjust the compact ParkModal layout so the park name remains readable while preserving the center-finding workflow and touch targets.
acceptance_criteria: In compact park mode, long park names remain readable on iPhone-width layouts; the strip does not visibly clip text against the viewport edge or controls; stop and rotation controls remain usable without overlapping the park name; regression verification is documented or covered by UI checks.

title: Start map at maximum zoom-in level
description: Set the initial map zoom to the same maximum zoom-in level already enforced for the map so users immediately see streets and paths on load.

title: Add autoplay regression coverage on main and debug routes
description: Update Playwright coverage to validate one-time unlock, auto-play on park entry, delayed-load auto-start, stale-load cancellation, and fallback behavior on the supported mobile profiles. Acceptance: tests cover / and /#/debug; worst-case loading still resolves into correct auto-play behavior; iPhone HTTPS run is part of validation.

title: Refactor park audio UI from play control to status/fallback
description: Remove the play button as the primary interaction in HOARenderer / ParkModal, replace it with loading/playing/error status, and keep only a fallback recovery start control for blocked/failed cases during the rollout. Acceptance: normal flow requires no per-park play tap; stop remains available; fallback start appears only when autoplay is blocked or unlock failed.

title: Fix Gimbal instance stored in useState instead of useRef
description: GimbalArrow stores the Gimbal utility instance in useState(). Mutable utility objects that don't drive rendering should use useRef, not useState. Storing it in state causes unnecessary React overhead and signals wrong intent.
close_reason: Fixed. Also added window.__gimbalOrientation observable and gimbal-orientation.spec.ts test with continuous rotation loop to verify spatial audio panning.

title: Delete orphaned GyroscopePermissionProvider and useDeviceOrientation
description: GyroscopePermissionProvider context and useDeviceOrientation hook are defined but never used anywhere in the codebase. GimbalArrow has its own local permission logic that covers the same concern. Delete both files to remove dead code.
close_reason: Deleted both files. Neither was imported anywhere outside itself.

title: Consolidate gimbal state split between HOARenderer and GimbalArrow
description: showGimbalArrow lives in HOARenderer and permissionGranted lives in GimbalArrow — related state split across two components. Consolidate so gimbal visibility and permission status have a single owner, removing the awkward coupling between parent and child.
close_reason: permissionGranted lifted to HOARenderer; GimbalArrow now receives permissionGranted + onPermissionGranted as props; both states reset on parkName change

title: Smooth location dot movement
description: Investigate how RGeolocation position updates are applied. Add position smoothing — interpolate between GPS fixes or use OpenLayers view animation. Add dead-zone threshold so map only re-centers on meaningful movement. Test with Playwright path replay.

title: Smooth map pan/zoom transitions
description: Audit RMap view update calls in OpenLayers.tsx. Add duration to view.animate() or view.setCenter() calls so transitions are animated. Check heading changes cause smooth rotation, not jerky jumps.

title: Body-Orientation Rotation Feedback
description: Replace checkbox with a fullscreen ambient gradient layer that responds to compass heading in real time. Linked with ParkModal redesign.

title: Modal Redesign — Clean/Minimal
description: Redesign WelcomeModal, HelpModal, and ParkModal with clean white backgrounds, generous whitespace, modern typography.

title: Map Tiles — Watercolor Style
description: Switch to Stadia Stamen Watercolor tiles for an artistic, impressionistic map background.

title: Proximity Approach Indicator
description: Three-phase proximity UX: (1) map marker glows as user approaches from afar, (2) directional indicator inside modal points toward center once in range, (3) AmbientGradient takes over at center for orientation. Replaces the LeavesCanvas animation.

title: Smooth Map & Location Tracking
description: Map panning and location dot feel janky while walking. Make movement continuous and smooth.

title: Clean up on-map UI elements
description: Audit all fixed-position overlays in OpenLayers.tsx. Debug panel: ensure it blends with watercolor background (adjust bg-white/92 opacity if needed). Help button and map controls: restyle to match clean/minimal system. Location dot marker: upgrade from PNG (geolocation_marker_heading.png) to SVG with clean minimal style. Park marker icons (trees.png): evaluate whether they read well over watercolor tiles — may need redesign. Use /frontend-design skill.

title: Redesign WelcomeModal
description: Replace boilerplate Headless UI dialog styling in WelcomeModal.tsx. Clean layout: app name as large light-weight heading, body text with comfortable line-height, single clear CTA button. Button: solid dark (near-black) fill, white text, rounded — not current outlined gray style. Keep existing Transition animations. Use /frontend-design skill.

title: Redesign HelpModal
description: Same design system as WelcomeModal redesign. Structure troubleshooting tips as short scannable list rather than paragraphs. Style contact email link to match new palette. Use /frontend-design skill.

title: Map Overlay UI Cleanup
description: Clean up all fixed-position overlays on the map: debug panel, controls, markers, icons.
